etymology.description: semantic 艹 (cǎo, 'grass; vegetation') + phonetic 新 (xīn, 'new') -> firewood
etymology.interpretation: 薪 (xīn, 'firewood') combines the semantic component 艹 (cǎo, 'grass; vegetation'), indicating a plant-related material, with the phonetic 新 (xīn, 'new'), which suggests the pronunciation. This form intuitively links the cutting of fresh plants or wood to create firewood.

examples.chinese: 农夫每天收集柴薪(農夫每天收集柴薪)。
examples.english: The farmer collects firewood every day.

examples.chinese: 冬天来临时(冬天來臨時)，人们需要更多的薪(人們需要更多的薪)。
examples.pinyin: Dōngtiān láilín shí, rénmen xūyào gèng duō de xīn.
examples.english: When winter arrives, people need more firewood.

examples.chinese: 他在山上砍了很多薪(他在山上砍了很多薪)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài shānshàng kǎnle hěn duō xīn.
examples.english: He chopped a lot of firewood on the mountain.
